_meta.purpose: Sub-industry taxonomy for recruitment/staffing icebreaker matrix
_meta.usage: LLM picks sub_industry from this list. Code does direct lookup in matrix.
_meta.note: Values are snake_case keys. Labels are human-readable for prompt injection.

sub_industries.hospitals: Hospital systems and large health networks
sub_industries.home_care: Home health, assisted living, senior care
sub_industries.health_tech: Digital health and health IT companies

sub_industries.cybersecurity: Cybersecurity firms and managed security services
sub_industries.ai_ml: AI, machine learning, data science companies
sub_industries.it_services: Managed IT services, IT staffing, tech consulting
sub_industries.ecommerce_tech: Technology platforms for ecommerce and retail

sub_industries.accounting: CPA firms, audit and tax advisory

sub_industries.real_estate_development: Real estate developers and property managers

sub_industries.virtual_assistants: VA placement and remote staffing for SMBs

services.executive_search: C-level, VP, and senior leadership hiring
services.sourcing: Candidate sourcing and pipeline research only

business_models.retained: Client pays upfront fee; used mostly in executive search
business_models.contingency: Fee paid only on successful placement
business_models.contract: Ongoing staffing contracts with client companies

company_type._note: High-level type of CLIENT companies the recruiter serves. Use client_profile for precise routing.
company_type.smb: Small/mid-size PRIVATE businesses, 10-250 employees (family-owned, regional, independent — not VC-backed)
company_type.mid_market: Mid-size companies, 250-1000 employees, often PE-backed or private growth stage
company_type.enterprise: Large corporations, 1000+ employees, Fortune 500, publicly traded
company_type.vc_backed: VC-backed or investor-funded high-growth companies (tech, biotech, fintech) — use client_profile to refine

client_profile._note: Coded compound descriptor — primary routing key for matrix lookup. More specific than company_type.
client_profile._routing: matrix.get((service, sub_industry, client_profile)) → matrix.get((service, sub_industry, company_type)) → matrix.get((service, company_type))
client_profile.vc_early: Seed or Series A, still finding PMF, lean team
client_profile.vc_growth: Series B/C, scaling fast, hiring urgently
client_profile.vc_late_ipo: Series D+, pre-IPO or recently public, board pressure on every hire
client_profile.vc_established: Mature VC-backed, no clear stage signal
client_profile.smb_trades: Contractors, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, construction trades
client_profile.smb_professional: Accounting firms, law firms, consultancies, agencies
client_profile.smb_healthcare: Private clinics, dental practices, boutique medical offices
client_profile.smb_general: General small business, no clear vertical
client_profile.nonprofit_small: Small nonprofits, community orgs, local foundations
client_profile.nonprofit_large: National nonprofits, large associations, major foundations
client_profile.mixed_types: Genuinely serves multiple distinct company profiles

company_stage._note: Only relevant when client_profile starts with vc_. For all others use not_applicable.
company_stage.seed: Pre-seed or seed stage, typically <$5M raised
company_stage.series_a_b: Series A or B, product-market fit, scaling team
company_stage.pre_ipo: Preparing for public listing, IPO imminent
company_stage.established: Mature VC-backed, stage unclear or not relevant
company_stage.not_applicable: client_profile does not start with vc_

geography._note: Geographic scope of the recruiting firm's placements. Default assumption = national (US) if unknown
geography.local: Specific US region, state, or metro area (e.g. Texas, NYC, Southeast)
geography.unknown: Not specified — treat as national for fallback purposes

fallback_chain: (service, secondary_industry)  -- if primary == multi_industry, try first secondary
